title: Introduction to Computer Programming
notes: A common first programming course. It is usually the starting point for students with little or no coding background.
warnings: Do not assume it is easy just because it is introductory.
Check current prerequisites, exclusions, and program rules officially.

title: Introduction to Computer Science
notes: Often treated as a key next programming course after CSC108 or equivalent background.
warnings: This can be a heavy course if your programming foundation is shaky.
Verify current POSt and prerequisite rules before planning around it.

title: Mathematical Expression and Reasoning for Computer Science
notes: Proofs, logic, and mathematical reasoning are central. The workload can feel different from programming-heavy CS courses.
warnings: Students sometimes underestimate the proof-writing adjustment.
Verify whether CSC165, CSC240, or another option fits your target program.

title: Calculus I
notes: A common first calculus option. Pace and assessment style can still be a jump from high school.
warnings: Check whether your intended program requires MAT135/136, MAT137, or a different calculus stream.

title: Calculus II
notes: Common continuation after MAT135, with integration and series-style topics depending on the offering.
warnings: Confirm prerequisites and whether the MAT135/136 stream satisfies your program requirements.

title: Calculus with Proofs
notes: A proof-oriented calculus course. It can be rewarding, but it is a major workload commitment for many first-year students.
warnings: Do not choose it only because it sounds stronger; check fit, background, and program needs.
Difficulty is highly subjective and instructor-dependent.

title: Linear Algebra I
notes: Introduces linear algebra ideas used across math, stats, economics, CS, and data-heavy paths.
warnings: Check whether your program expects MAT223, MAT240, or another linear algebra option.

title: Introduction to Statistical Reasoning and Data Science
notes: A common first statistics course for students exploring data, statistics, or quantitative programs.
warnings: Verify current statistics program requirements and exclusions before relying on it.

title: Modern Symbolic Logic
notes: Logic-focused course that may appeal to students who like formal reasoning.
warnings: It is not a replacement for CS or math requirements unless official sources say so.

title: The Sun and Its Neighbours
notes: Often considered an accessible science breadth option, but workload depends on the offering.
warnings: Check breadth category, assessment format, and exclusions in the current Calendar.

title: Principles of Microeconomics
notes: A standard first economics course and common starting point for economics paths.
warnings: Program entry rules can be specific; verify grades, combinations, and exclusions officially.

title: Principles of Macroeconomics
notes: Common companion to ECO101 for students considering economics or related programs.
warnings: Check whether ECO101 and ECO102 are both needed for your intended program.
